questions.question: What is the chemical symbol for water?
questions.explanation: Water is H2O — two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.

questions.question: The chemical formula of sodium chloride (common salt) is:
questions.explanation: Sodium chloride (common salt) has the formula NaCl — one sodium (Na) and one chlorine (Cl) atom.

questions.question: The chemical name of 'washing soda' is:
questions.explanation: Washing soda is hydrated sodium carbonate (Na2CO3·10H2O). Baking soda is sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO3).

questions.question: Rusting of iron is a chemical process called:
questions.explanation: Rusting is the corrosion of iron. It is an oxidation reaction: 4Fe + 3O2 + 6H2O → 4Fe(OH)3 (hydrated iron oxide). Moisture and oxygen are needed.

questions.question: Which enzyme in the saliva begins the digestion of starch?
questions.explanation: Salivary amylase (ptyalin) begins the chemical digestion of starch in the mouth, breaking it into maltose.

questions.question: Which organelle is responsible for protein synthesis?
questions.explanation: Ribosomes are the sites of protein synthesis (translation). They read mRNA sequences and assemble amino acids into proteins.
